solve(nums, queries) ->
    % Preprocessing: Calculate prefix sums S
    % S[i] = count of even numbers in nums[0..i]
    S = calculate_prefix_sums(nums),
    
    % Process each query
    Ans = lists:map(fun([L, R, K]) -> solve_query(nums, S, L, R, K) end, queries),
    Ans.

%% Helper to calculate prefix sums of even numbers
calculate_prefix_sums(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    % S is represented as a list where S[i] is the prefix count up to index i
    % We use fold/reduce pattern for O(N) calculation
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) ->
        CurrentCount = case lists:last(Acc) of
                        [] -> 0;
                        LastVal -> LastVal
                    end,
        
        IsEven = (mod(X, 2) == 0),
        NewCount = CurrentCount + (if IsEven -> 1; true -> 0 end),
        [NewCount | Acc]
    end, [])(Nums),
    % The fold accumulates in reverse order, so reverse it back
    lists:reverse(lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) ->
        CurrentCount = case lists:last(Acc) of
                        [] -> 0;
                        LastVal -> LastVal
                    end,
        
        IsEven = (mod(X, 2) == 0),
        NewCount = CurrentCount + (if IsEven -> 1; true -> 0 end),
        [NewCount | Acc]
    end, [])(Nums)).

%% Helper to find the largest index i such that Nums[i] <= X
% Returns -1 if no such element exists.
find_max_index_le(Nums, X) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    % Binary search implementation
    search(0, N - 1, -1),
    where
        search(Low, High, BestIndex) when Low > High ->
            BestIndex;
        search(Low, High, BestIndex) ->
            Mid = (Low + High) div 2,
            
            case Nums[Mid] of
                Value when Value =< X ->
                    % This is a possible candidate. Try finding a larger index.
                    search(Mid + 1, High, Mid);
                _ ->
                    % Value is too large. Look in the left half.
                    search(Low, Mid - 1, BestIndex)
            end.

%% Helper to calculate C(X): count of even numbers in Nums[L..R] <= X
% S is the prefix sum array (0-indexed, length N)
calculate_C(Nums, S, L, R, X) ->
    % 1. Find R_X: largest index i such that Nums[i] <= X
    R_X = find_max_index_le(Nums, X),
    
    % 2. Determine effective upper bound R'
    R_Prime = min(R, R_X),
    
    % 3. Calculate count using prefix sums S
    if R_Prime < L ->
        0;
    true ->
        % S[R_Prime] is count up to R_Prime
        Count_R_Prime = S[R_Prime],
        
        % S[L-1] is count up to L-1. If L=0, this is 0.
        Count_L_Minus_1 = if L == 0 -> 0; S[L - 1] -> true end,
        
        Count_R_Prime - Count_L_Minus_1
    end.

%% Helper to check condition F(X) >= K
check(Nums, S, L, R, X, K) ->
    C_X = calculate_C(Nums, S, L, R, X),
    
    % F(X) = X/2 - C(X)
    % Since X is guaranteed even by the BS loop structure, division by 2 is safe.
    F_X = X div 2 - C_X,
    
    F_X >= K.

%% Solves a single query using binary search
solve_query(Nums, S, L, R, K) ->
    % Search range for X (must be even)
    L_bs = 2,
    % Max K is 10^9, max removal count is 10^5. X_max approx 2*10^9 + 10^5. 3*10^9 is safe upper bound.
    H_bs = 3000000000, 
    
    % Binary search implementation to find smallest X such that check(X) is true
    search_bs(L_bs, H_bs, H_bs), % Initialize result to H_bs (a safe upper bound if K is huge)
    where
        search_bs(Low, High, BestX) when Low > High ->
            BestX;
        search_bs(Low, High, BestX) ->
            % Since we are searching over all integers, we find the smallest integer X*.
            % We only care about the smallest EVEN X* satisfying the condition.
            
            Mid = Low + (High - Low) div 2,
            
            % Ensure Mid is even for consistency in F(X) calculation
            X = if mod(Mid, 2) == 0 -> Mid; Mid + 1 -> true end,

            % We must check X, which is the smallest even number >= Mid
            
            case check(Nums, S, L, R, X, K) of
                true ->
                    % X satisfies the condition. Store it and try smaller values.
                    search_bs(Low, X - 2, X); % Next search starts at X-2 (next smaller even)
                false ->
                    % X is too small. Need a larger X.
                    search_bs(X + 2, High, BestX) % Next search starts at X+2 (next larger even)
            end.

solve(nums, queries) ->
    % Preprocessing: Calculate prefix sums S
    % S[i] = count of even numbers in nums[0..i]
    S = calculate_prefix_sums(nums),
    
    % Process each query
    Ans = lists:map(fun([L, R, K]) -> solve_query(nums, S, L, R, K) end, queries),
    Ans.

%% Helper to calculate prefix sums of even numbers
calculate_prefix_sums(Nums) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    % S is represented as a list where S[i] is the prefix count up to index i
    % We use fold/reduce pattern for O(N) calculation
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) ->
        CurrentCount = case lists:last(Acc) of
                        [] -> 0;
                        LastVal -> LastVal
                    end,
        
        IsEven = (mod(X, 2) == 0),
        NewCount = CurrentCount + (if IsEven -> 1; true -> 0 end),
        [NewCount | Acc]
    end, [])(Nums),
    % The fold accumulates in reverse order, so reverse it back
    lists:reverse(lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) ->
        CurrentCount = case lists:last(Acc) of
                        [] -> 0;
                        LastVal -> LastVal
                    end,
        
        IsEven = (mod(X, 2) == 0),
        NewCount = CurrentCount + (if IsEven -> 1; true -> 0 end),
        [NewCount | Acc]
    end, [])(Nums)).

%% Helper to find the largest index i such that Nums[i] <= X
% Returns -1 if no such element exists.
find_max_index_le(Nums, X) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    % Binary search implementation
    search(0, N - 1, -1),
    where
        search(Low, High, BestIndex) when Low > High ->
            BestIndex;
        search(Low, High, BestIndex) ->
            Mid = (Low + High) div 2,
            
            case Nums[Mid] of
                Value when Value =< X ->
                    % This is a possible candidate. Try finding a larger index.
                    search(Mid + 1, High, Mid);
                _ ->
                    % Value is too large. Look in the left half.
                    search(Low, Mid - 1, BestIndex)
            end.

%% Helper to calculate C(X): count of even numbers in Nums[L..R] <= X
% S is the prefix sum array (0-indexed, length N)
calculate_C(Nums, S, L, R, X) ->
    % 1. Find R_X: largest index i such that Nums[i] <= X
    R_X = find_max_index_le(Nums, X),
    
    % 2. Determine effective upper bound R'
    R_Prime = min(R, R_X),
    
    % 3. Calculate count using prefix sums S
    if R_Prime < L ->
        0;
    true ->
        % S[R_Prime] is count up to R_Prime
        Count_R_Prime = S[R_Prime],
        
        % S[L-1] is count up to L-1. If L=0, this is 0.
        Count_L_Minus_1 = if L == 0 -> 0; S[L - 1] -> true end,
        
        Count_R_Prime - Count_L_Minus_1
    end.

%% Helper to check condition F(X) >= K
check(Nums, S, L, R, X, K) ->
    C_X = calculate_C(Nums, S, L, R, X),
    
    % F(X) = X/2 - C(X)
    % Since X is guaranteed even by the BS loop structure, division by 2 is safe.
    F_X = X div 2 - C_X,
    
    F_X >= K.

%% Solves a single query using binary search
solve_query(Nums, S, L, R, K) ->
    % Search range for X (must be even)
    L_bs = 2,
    % Max K is 10^9, max removal count is 10^5. X_max approx 2*10^9 + 10^5. 3*10^9 is safe upper bound.
    H_bs = 3000000000, 
    
    % Binary search implementation to find smallest X such that check(X) is true
    search_bs(L_bs, H_bs, H_bs), % Initialize result to H_bs (a safe upper bound if K is huge)
    where
        search_bs(Low, High, BestX) when Low > High ->
            BestX;
        search_bs(Low, High, BestX) ->
            % Since we are searching over all integers, we find the smallest integer X*.
            % We only care about the smallest EVEN X* satisfying the condition.
            
            Mid = Low + (High - Low) div 2,
            
            % Ensure Mid is even for consistency in F(X) calculation
            X = if mod(Mid, 2) == 0 -> Mid; Mid + 1 -> true end,

            % We must check X, which is the smallest even number >= Mid
            
            case check(Nums, S, L, R, X, K) of
                true ->
                    % X satisfies the condition. Store it and try smaller values.
                    search_bs(Low, X - 2, X); % Next search starts at X-2 (next smaller even)
                false ->
                    % X is too small. Need a larger X.
                    search_bs(X + 2, High, BestX) % Next search starts at X+2 (next larger even)
            end.

where

    % PE[i] stores the count of even numbers in Nums[0..i]

        EvenCount = if (Num rem 2 == 0) -> 1; 0;
        NewCount = case Acc of
            [] -> [EvenCount];
            [LastCount|Rest] -> [LastCount + EvenCount | Rest]

% Since Erlang lists are naturally head-recursive/LIFO, storing the prefix sums

                Num when Num > X ->

solve(Nums, Queries) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    
    % 1. Precompute P_even
    P_even = compute_p_even(Nums, N),
    
    % 2. Process queries
    Ans = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Query) ->
        [L, R, K] = Query,
        Result = solve_query(Nums, P_even, N, L, R, K),
        [Result | Acc]
    end, [], Queries),
    
    lists:reverse(Ans).

%% =====================================================================
%% Helper functions for Precomputation
%% =====================================================================

% compute_p_even(Nums, N) calculates P_even array of size N+1.
% P_even[i] stores the count of even numbers in Nums[0..i-1].
compute_p_even(Nums, N) ->
    % Initialize P_even[0] = 0
    P_even_init = [0],
    % Iterate through Nums using foldl to build the list iteratively
    P_even = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Num) ->
        Last_Count = lists:last(Acc),
        Is_Even = (mod(Num, 2) == 0),
        New_Count = Last_Count + (if Is_Even -> 1; true -> 0 end),
        [New_Count | Acc]
    end, P_even_init, Nums),
    lists:reverse(P_even). 

%% =====================================================================
%% Helper functions for Binary Search (Upper Bound)
%% =====================================================================

% upper_bound(Nums, X) returns the smallest index i such that Nums[i] > X, or N.
upper_bound(Nums, X) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    binary_search_upper_bound(Nums, X, 0, N - 1, N).

% Helper to safely get element from list by 0-based index
element(I, L) when I >= 0 and I < length(L) ->
    lists:nth(I + 1, L);
element(_, _) ->
    error(index_out).

% Binary search implementation for upper bound
binary_search_upper_bound(Nums, X, Low, High, Default) when Low > High ->
    Default;
binary_search_upper_bound(Nums, X, Low, High, Default) ->
    Mid = (Low + High) div 2,
    
    case element(Mid, Nums) of
        Element when Element > X ->
            % Mid is a potential answer. Try to find a smaller index.
            Result = binary_search_upper_bound(Nums, X, Low, Mid - 1, Mid);
            Result;
        Element when Element =< X ->
            % Mid is too small or just right. Search right.
            binary_search_upper_bound(Nums, X, Mid + 1, High, Default)
    end.

%% =====================================================================
%% Query Solver
%% =====================================================================

% solve_query handles one specific query [L, R, K]
solve_query(Nums, P_even, N, L, R, K) ->
    % Binary search for J such that J - D(2J) >= K
    
    % Initial range for J: J >= K (minimum possible value)
    L_j = K,
    % Safe upper bound: K + N + 2. Since D(2J) >= 0, J <= K + N.
    R_j = K + N + 2, 
    
    J_ans = binary_search_J(Nums, P_even, N, L, R, K, L_j, R_j),
    
    2 * J_ans.

% Binary search for the smallest J satisfying the condition
binary_search_J(Nums, P_even, N, L, R, K, Low, High) when Low > High ->
    error(search_failed); % Should not be reached if K >= 1
    
binary_search_J(Nums, P_even, N, L, R, K, Low, High) ->
    J = (Low + High) div 2,
    
    X = 2 * J,
    
    % I_upper: Index of first element > X. Range [0, N].
    I_upper = upper_bound(Nums, X),
    
    % R_prime: The effective upper index for the range [L, R] intersected with elements <= X.
    R_prime = min(R, I_upper - 1),
    
    D_X = 0,
    if R_prime >= L ->
        % Count of evens in Nums[L .. R_prime]
        % Count = P_even[R_prime + 1] - P_even[L]
        Count_R_prime_plus_1 = element(R_prime + 1, P_even),
        Count_L = element(L, P_even),
        D_X = Count_R_prime_plus_1 - Count_L
    end;
    
    C = J - D_X,
    
    if C >= K ->
        % J is sufficient. Record J and search for a smaller possible J.
        J_ans = binary_search_J(Nums, P_even, N, L, R, K, Low, J);
        J_ans
    else ->
        % J is too small. Search in the upper half.
        binary_search_J(Nums, P_even, N, L, R, K, J + 1, High)
    end.

%% Helper functions for Precomputation
%% =====================================================================

% compute_p_even(Nums, N) calculates P_even array of size N+1.
% P_even[i] stores the count of even numbers in Nums[0..i-1].
compute_p_even(Nums, N) ->
    % Initialize P_even[0] = 0
    P_even_init = [0],
    % Iterate through Nums using foldl to build the list iteratively
    P_even = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Num) ->
        Last_Count = lists:last(Acc),
        Is_Even = (mod(Num, 2) == 0),
        New_Count = Last_Count + (if Is_Even -> 1; true -> 0 end),
        [New_Count | Acc]
    end, P_even_init, Nums),
    lists:reverse(P_even).

%% =====================================================================

% upper_bound(Nums, X) returns the smallest index i such that Nums[i] > X, or N.
upper_bound(Nums, X) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    binary_search_upper_bound(Nums, X, 0, N - 1, N).

% Helper to safely get element from list by 0-based index
element(I, L) when I >= 0 and I < length(L) ->

            Result;

solve_query(Nums, P_even, N, L, R, K) ->
    % Binary search for J such that J - D(2J) >= K
    
    % Initial range for J: J >= K (minimum possible value)
    L_j = K,

        J_ans = binary_search_J(Nums, P_even, N, L, R, K, Low, J);
        J_ans
    else ->
        % J is too small. Search in the upper half.
        binary_search_J(Nums, P_even, N, L, R, K, J + 1, High)
    end.
